headline: Cursor's Automations Reign Supreme: Self-Hosted Cloud Agents Go Live, Claude Code Auto Mode Drops, Copilot Speed Runs 50% Faster

stories.title: Cursor Self-Hosted Cloud Agents Now Live
stories.summary: Cursor dropped self-hosted cloud agents on Mar 25 so teams can keep all code execution within their own infrastructure—literally zero exposure. Agents get the same capabilities (isolated VMs, full dev environments, multi-model harnesses) but run locally.
stories.takeaway: Enterprise devs can now vibe code w/o leaving their network. Biggest security flex of the week.

stories.title: Copilot Coding Agent Now 50% Faster, Jira Integration Gets Model Picker
stories.summary: GitHub optimized startup times for Copilot agents (Mar 19) + shipped Jira integration enhancements w/ model selection & Confluence context access. Also deprecated Gemini 3 Pro across all Copilot surfaces.
stories.takeaway: Copilot's getting snappier. Model choice in Jira is lowkey the move for teams that live in Atlassian.

stories.title: Claude Code Auto Mode in Research Preview: Let AI Decide What's Safe
stories.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code auto mode (Mar 24) so the AI picks which actions are safe w/o asking permission first. It's gated by safety layers checking for prompt injection & unexpected behavior. Still in research preview.
stories.takeaway: Hot take: this is either the future of vibe coding or a 3am idea that'll haunt them. Safety details still fuzzy.

stories.title: The Pragmatic Engineer Survey: 95% of Devs Use AI Weekly, Claude Code Dominates
stories.summary: Survey of 906 engineers (Mar 7) shows 55% now regularly use AI agents (up from basically zero 18 months ago), Cursor growing 35% YoY, and Claude Code went from zero to most-used tool in 8 months. Opus/Sonnet models crush all others combined.
stories.takeaway: Claude Code's ascent is genuinely wild. Not surprising tho—terminal-native beats IDE lock-in every time for experienced devs.

watchNext.title: Simon Willison on Agentic Engineering: Architecture Over Vibes
watchNext.why: Drops real insight on why AI agents need better libraries/patterns, not just more tokens. His Pragmatic Summit fireside chat (Mar 14) reframes how senior devs should think about agent-assisted work.

watchNext.title: Anthropic Data Leak Reveals Claude Mythos: Next Tier AI Model Coming
watchNext.why: Oops moment (Mar 26): Anthropic accidentally exposed drafts of Claude Mythos (bigger than Opus, cheaper than current tier structure) + Capybara tier. Dual-use security concerns being taken seriously.
